# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// You will need to install the edge-config package using npm or yarn, then you can import and use its configuration here in your function 
import { readConfig } from 'edge-config';   // Assuming that it's installed as a dev dependency. In real production code be sure not to hardcode sensitive data like API_KEY etc into source codes directly instead fetch them using environment variables or any secure way you prefer, for instance with vercel’s secret management service 
import { defaultConfig } from './default-config'; // Assuming we have a ./default-config.js file where the configuration will be declared (you may create it if not already exists). This provides us some defaults that can work immediately in local development and then overwritten by processEnv at runtime according to env variables
import { connect } from 'pg'  // Connecting with PostgreSQL using pg package for NodeJS. Install this as a dependency via npm or yarn depending on your requirements if not already installed globally, which is required specifically when dealing with database operations in an AWS Lambda (serverless) environment  

export default async function(req: any): Promise<any> {  // Here we handle the request and response objects using Next.js’s req/res object model    . This will be your Edge Function code, where you can define what should happen when a new 'request' is made to this endpoint  
let config;              // Declare our configuration variable that holds all settings from edge-config 
try {                 
     await readConfig();      // Read the configurations using process.env in NodeJS    . This method will automatically use environment variables (Node’s version of Heroku's Config Vars) for setting defaults if not defined or are empty   , and save it to config variable after successfully reading all configuration files 
} catch {                  // Catch any error that might occur while trying the readConfig() call. This would usually happen when there is an issue with one of your environment variables in .env file, so just ignore this case by 'await'ing promise   }      const API_KEY = config?.API_KEY || processEnv('EDGE_CONFIG').REACT_APP__API_DEFAULT;   
const DB_URL=config.DB_Url||process.env['DB_url'] ||  "postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com" ;  // Defaulting to PostgreSql if not provided via edge config or environment variables   const db = connect({connectionString : new URL(DB_URL)});   
// The rest of your code will use the configured API KEY and DB connection.. Do note that you'll also need handle situations where NODE_ENV is 'production', which should then set `process.env` based on whether or not it’s a production environment   return db;  //Return database Connection for further operations }
